rows.source_ref: "1 Hour after daily open", author Nik13, ForexFactory thread #535657 (~2014, not explicitly dated in the excerpt). https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/535657/1-hour-after-daily-open
rows.concept: After the daily (Sydney) open, the close of the first H1 candle relative to the daily-open line signals directional continuation through the low-volatility pre-London session.
rows.eligibility_reason: Pure-MQL5 deterministic daily-open/first-hour-close breakout with fixed 10-pip SL/TP on EURUSD/GBPUSD; .DWX data available, has stops, FTMO/DXZ-compatible.
rows.notes: Base rules: buy if first H1 closes above the daily-open line, sell if below; SL=TP=10 pips; EURUSD+GBPUSD opened together with a basket equity-TP (close both when combined +10 pips). Symmetric 1:1 with very tight stops - author concedes SL likely hit >75% and member Mariodrugs reports a 2-week test as 'not worth'; edge doubtful but cheap to test as a session-structure driver. Optional martingale MM overlay (pages 12-13) is OUT OF SCOPE / incompatible with FTMO+DXZ. imran.78p's discretionary variant (DO +-15-20 entry, opposite-side stop, MA100/200 hourly filter, hold for the day) is VAGUE/manual and not carried as a separate row.

rows.source_ref: "1 Hour after daily open", author Nik13, ForexFactory thread #535657 (~2014, not explicitly dated in the excerpt); 'Another similar system' post. https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/535657/1-hour-after-daily-open
rows.concept: Fade the first post-open hour by placing a pending stop at the daily-open line opposite to the first H1 candle's direction, betting price re-crosses the open.
rows.eligibility_reason: Deterministic pending-stop re-cross of the daily-open line on FX majors; implementable in pure MQL5 with stops, but TP not firmly specified by the author.
rows.notes: Nik13's 'another similar system' (page 13): if the first H1 candle is bearish place a BUY-STOP at the daily-open line, if bullish place a SELL-STOP; SL 10, TP ~10 ('but not sure, may be more'). Distinct edge from Row 1 (re-cross/fade vs continuation), hence a separate row. No forward/backtest evidence provided in the thread.

notes: Forum thread, low reputability (anecdotal, no verified track record) - reflected in MED/LOW priority per harvest rules, not a REJECT. Two mechanical candidates split by trade logic (continuation market entry vs re-cross pending stop). Discretionary/martingale material folded into notes rather than separate rows.
